steps.title: Join two clauses
steps.content: The clause about animals dying out and the clause about the theory leaving a question unexplored are both independent clauses. They are joined by the coordinating conjunction but.

steps.title: Place the comma before but
steps.content: A omits the needed comma, C omits the conjunction, and D leaves only a comma between independent clauses. A comma before but correctly joins the two clauses. The answer is B.
